# Multi-window docking choreography — real OS windows on the shared heap

## Problem scope

The dense workstation shipped (apps/workstation, the `#15099`→`#15144` arc) and two-window transfer is real (`#14772`), but the release demo stops at the window edge. The wow bar — the reason this needs Neo rather than any web framework — is the five-beat filmable story ratified on D#15204:

1. **Tear-out:** drag a tab past the window edge → it becomes a real OS popup window, left where dropped.
2. **Second tear-out:** another tab converts to a popup **while dragging**.
3. **Popup-over-popup:** drag it over the first popup → dock-zone previews light **inside the other popup**.
4. **Dock:** release → both panes docked in one popup, still live (stores streaming, heartbeats never reset).
5. **Reintegration:** drag the merged stack back into main → previews light, drop commits, the emptied popup closes itself.

Every beat on the shared App-Worker heap — the same JavaScript instances throughout. Portal-style DOM tricks (Vue/React) cannot do this: they re-render into a foreign document; Neo *moves the living instance*. This is the engine-category differentiator the release recording leads with.

**Why an Epic:** the work spans the drag stack (`src/draggable`), the window manager (`Neo.manager.Window` / `DragCoordinator`), the dock model + reconcilers (`src/dashboard`), an ADR amendment, a three-OS portability spike, and multiple app consumers — ≥6 coordinated one-PR leaves with hard merge-order constraints. No single ticket can carry it.

## Intended solution shape

- **The landed lineage is the grammar:** `#7204` Dynamic Proxy Transitioning + `#8160` direction-aware hysteresis (`SortZone.checkWindowBoundary()`, 0.8 detach / 0.6 reattach) + the landed live-popup embodiment (`dashboard.Container#onDragBoundaryExit/Entry`, `DragDrop` pointer-follow) are REUSED, never parallel-built. The dock lifts its `enableProxyToPopup` opt-out into dock semantics.
- **Portability is measured, not assumed:** a headed three-OS spike emits the seven-row `PASS_NATIVE | PASS_FALLBACK | FAIL` matrix (hysteretic grammar · acquisition · moving embodiment · object permanence · screen topology · multi-window targeting · terminal cleanup); its RESULTS select implementation defaults. `windowOpen` returns a Boolean (a blocked popup never throws) — receipts assert it. **Evidence ownership:** macOS receipts = the spike's executing agent (the live sandbox is darwin); Windows + Linux receipts = operator-produced headed runs (human-owned hardware — a Tier-4 handoff with the receipt-intake checklist carried on `#15243`); the matrix is complete only when all three OS rows carry live receipts.
- **Determinism is contract, not registration order:** hover arbitration = a session-scoped gesture/claim protocol on stable workspace/zone identity (deterministic tie / stale / no-claim, fail closed; a ≥3-window overlap witness proves exactly one preview + one commit). The gesture-to-effect boundary is a finite outcome machine (`IN_SOURCE → DETACHED_MOVING → HOVERING_CLAIM → {COMMITTED_TARGET | TERMINAL_DETACHED | REJECTED | CANCELLED}`) — source cleanup and vessel close only after `COMMITTED_TARGET`; commit precedes close; everything idempotent exact-once.
- **Authority rides an ADR amendment:** `Decision Record: REQUIRED — amends ADR 0029` (no new ADR, no supersede) in a separate merge-ordered PR owning arbitration, outcome states, and vessel lifecycle — landing BEFORE any consuming implementation.
- **Ownership boundary (operator-ratified 2026-07-16):** shared docking logic lives in `src/dashboard` as app-neutral owners; `apps/agentos` (FM + future harness) and `apps/workstation` (the standalone flagship demo) are thin consumers; theming flows through the `--agent-dock-preview-*` domain aliases each app projects (PR `#14955` / PR `#15208` boundary; `#15144`'s no-AgentOS/no-FM workstation contract preserved).
- **Authority/consumption order (write-surface map; truth-folded 2026-07-16 after PR `#15260` merged):** the ADR 0029 §2.8 amendment (`#15240`) is **DELIVERED** — authority landed before every consumer, as prescribed. Now-parallel readiness rails under that authority: the three-OS spike (`#15243`) and the shared-affordance owner (`#15207`) proceed independently. Consumption then follows the edge graph: G1 (`#15244`, defaults gated on the spike's measured matrix) + G2 (`#15245`, platform acquisition defaults) → G3 (`#15246`, composition/claim arbitration) → G4 (`#15247` whole-stack return, consuming `#15248`'s exact-once terminal invariants) → consumers (`#15250` keyboard parity; `#15251` cockpit Stage-2, narrowed post-PR `#15266`) → the wow-demo leaf (`#15252`). Portal registration (`#15241`) rides workstation-done (`#15207`). Placement hints stay additive on `dockLayout.v2`.

**Unresolved Liveness (carried from the Discussion):** OQ4 — the design-language pick (operator side-by-side, post PR `#15208`; skin-tier only) · OQ5a — stack source projection vs `transferNode()` root rejection (G4's first design task; additive `dockLayout.v2` only) · OQ7 — multi-screen assumptions (the spike's row-5 contract; `getScreenDetails` never a prerequisite).

**revalidationTrigger:** reopen D#15204 and pause consuming implementation if the spike matrix returns `FAIL` for any universal invariant (gesture continuity, same-instance permanence, JSON-only persisted state, exact-once commit, idempotent cleanup); if the ADR 0029 amendment cannot preserve the folded claim/outcome contracts; or if OQ5a requires non-additive `dockLayout.v2` schema evolution.

**Criteria mapping:** D#15204 cycles 1–4c resolved OQ1/OQ2/OQ3/OQ5b/OQ6/OQ8 to `[RESOLVED_TO_AC]` contracts; those ACs land in the leaf tickets (per epic-create, this body carries none).

## Out of scope

In-window docking polish (the shipped `#14974`/`#14772` family) · the G5 design-language SELECTION itself (rides `#15206` + OQ4, operator's eye) · FM cockpit surfaces (the `#14610` successor CONSUMES the G1/G2 seam as first contracted consumer; it does not extend this epic) · Electron shell materialization (ADR 0034 may improve vessels, never fork placement/arbitration semantics) · amending `#13158`/`#14789` (deliberately parented standalone; both stay `Related`).

## Avoided traps (divergence preserved from D#15204)

- **Release-time `window.open`** — WHATWG activation expires; retained only as the matrix's negative control.
- **`windowId`/registration-order target identity** — the falsified `Window.getWindowAt()` first-intersecting behavior; the claim contract replaces it.
- **A second drag system / dwell coordinator** — the landed `#13028` 450ms/250ms native-popup candidate path is extended, never duplicated.
- **Verbatim cross-app porting** — PR `#15237`'s lesson: precedent spanning app ownership + async lifecycle lifts into the shared owner (`src/dashboard`), never copies across an app boundary.
- **Silent re-scoping of adjacent epics** — `#13158` (in-window) and `#14789` (four-beat fusion tour) keep their own scopes.
- **Relaxing the global root-transfer guard** to implement whole-stack return — the additive projection (OQ5a, `#15247`) is the only path; `transferNode()`'s root rejection stays intact.
- **Private target registries or vessel state machines in consumers** — keyboard (`#15250`) and cockpit (`#15251`) consume `#15246`'s stable registry and the shell-owned vessel machine; a second path recreates the arbitration problem it exists to solve.
- **Video/frames as evidence** — recordings are release derivatives; the executable worker-truth journey (`#15252`) is the only authoritative proof.

## Related

Source: D#15204 (graduated; anchors above) · ADR 0029 (amended by a leaf) · ADR 0020/0034 · `#13158` · `#14789` · `#15144` · PR `#14772` / PR `#15193` / `#13028` / `#8160` / `#7204` (the lineage) · `#15206` / `#15207` (re-parented leaves).

### @neo-gpt-emmy - 2026-07-16T11:46:45Z

## Epic Review by Emmy (GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra, Codex)

### Stage 1 — Roadmap Fit

✅

The multi-window choreography is a distinct v13.2 release lane: it advances the real-OS-window/shared-heap differentiator without absorbing the adjacent in-window docking or Fleet cockpit epics. The live parent/sub graph contains no competing epic for this five-beat journey.

### Stage 2 — Approach Elegance

✅

The shape reuses the landed popup-transition lineage (#7204, #8160, #13025/#13028), current 0.8/0.6 hysteresis, DragCoordinator, and the dashboard model instead of inventing a second windowing or drag substrate. VBA/Infinite Canvas provide the 50%-boundary and reintegration ancestry; they do not override the current calibrated thresholds. ADR 0029 remains the right authority surface.

### Stage 2.5 — Source Discussion Criteria Mapping Gate

✅

The repaired `## Discussion Criteria Mapping` now carries every D#15204 criterion into named leaves, preserves the required ADR 0029 amendment, records delivered #15206, and retains the liveness/revalidation triggers. This closes my prior Stage-2.5 blocker.

### Stage 3 — Sub-Structure Coherence

❌ — the leaf architecture is coherent, but the live bodies and relationship graph do not yet encode the prescribed consumption order reliably.

Required repairs:

1. **Truth-fold the parent order.** The parent still says `spike → settle #15207 → ADR amendment`, but #15240/PR #15260 is already landed while #15243 and #15207 remain open. Rewrite this as the actual authority/consumption order, with #15240 marked delivered and the now-parallel readiness rails named.
2. **Truth-fold #15207.** Its top authority correction prescribes the shared `src/dashboard` owner, but the lower live body still says `Parent #13158`, excludes multi-window/tear-out affordances, prescribes AgentOS/`--fm-*` reuse, and carries the superseded workstation-only fix. The correction cannot coexist with contradictory active sections.
3. **Repair relationship drift.**
   - #15241 says it is gated on #15207, but has no `blockedBy` edge.
   - #15251 says `blocked-by #15244 + #15245 + #15212`; the live graph omits #15245.
   - #15247 consumes #15248's exact-once terminal invariants, but has no #15248 predecessor.
   - #15250's target enumeration/visual selection consumes #15246's stable registry and #15207's shared affordance owner; neither edge exists.
   - #15252 consumes G2 acquisition, shared affordances, and exact terminal cleanup; add #15245, #15207, and #15248 (or document an equivalent parallel-build/merge-before-consumer contract).
4. **Name three-OS evidence ownership.** #15243 requires macOS, Windows, and Linux live receipts; the epic currently names no producer/handoff for the platforms this agent sandbox cannot supply.

No dependency cycle was found.

#### Stage 3.1 — Closeout Matrix (entry-seeded)

| Parent criterion | Required evidence | Owning sub(s) | Delivered PR(s) | Achieved evidence | Residual state |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| OQ1 — hysteretic dock tear-out grammar | L2 reducer/terminal contract + L3 real pointer tear-out/re-entry | #15244 | (pending) | (pending) | (pending) |
| OQ2/OQ7 — seven-row portability matrix, including screen topology | L3 headed receipts on macOS, Windows, Linux | #15243 | (pending) | (pending) | platform ownership pending |
| OQ3 — deterministic gesture/claim arbitration | L2 tie/stale/no-claim matrix + L3 ≥3-window overlap | #15240, #15246 | #15260 (authority) | ADR text landed | implementation pending |
| OQ5a — additive whole-stack source projection | L2 schema/migration/transaction proofs + L3 whole-stack return | #15247 | (pending) | (pending) | (pending) |
| OQ5b — exact-once terminal cleanup and vessel close | L2 four-terminal cleanup matrix + L3 mid-gesture disconnect/close-after-commit | #15248, #15247 | (pending) | (pending) | (pending) |
| OQ8 — keyboard/a11y parity | L2 command/a11y snapshot + L3 real-popup focus transfer | #15250 | (pending) | (pending) | (pending) |
| OQ4/G5 — selected preview language on shared owner | L2 generation/cancel/release-truth proofs + L3 real pointer flagship journey | #15206, #15207 | #15208 (language candidate) | candidate delivered | shared owner pending |
| Boolean admission truth | L2 Boolean failure paths + L3 blocked/opened popup receipts | #15240, #15243, #15245 | #15260 (authority) | ADR text landed | platform/default handling pending |
| Cockpit contracted consumer | L2 seam contract + L3 same-instance/live-stream round-trip | #15251, #14610 | PR #15266 pending terminal review | (pending) | narrow after PR outcome |
| Five-beat North Star | L3 one-run real-pointer worker-truth journey; recording is derivative evidence | #15252 | (pending) | (pending) | (pending) |
| Portal registration | L1 catalog contract + L2 portal smoke | #15241 | (pending) | (pending) | (pending) |

### Stage 4 — Prescription Layer

⚠️

- **#15248 is correctly prescribed.** Current `DragCoordinator` still retires the source after a null remote commit and `unregister()` can leave `activeTargetZone` live; coordinator-level outcome-aware teardown is the right repair.
- **#15247 needs its OQ5a descriptor pinned before implementation.** Keep the global root-transfer rejection intact; define the additive whole-stack projection rather than weakening `transferNode()` to make the leaf convenient.
- **#15250 must consume #15246's workspace/zone registry.** A keyboard-only private target registry would recreate the arbitration problem in a second path.
- **#15251 has an authority delta.** PR #15266 already proposes the Stage-1 shell-owned vessel state machine, owner accessor, preserved park, exact-index reattach, and two-window witness. After its terminal outcome, narrow #15251 to the Stage-2 gesture consumption of the epic seam; do not redeliver those mechanics.
- **#15252 belongs in the whitebox e2e layer.** The executable worker-truth journey is authoritative. Any novel recording script, rather than a sibling-spec lift, must run structural pre-flight separately.

### Stage 5 — Avoided Traps Completeness

⚠️

The current avoided-traps section is strong. Add three explicit guards:

- Never relax the global root-transfer guard to implement whole-stack return.
- Keyboard and cockpit consumers must not invent private target registries or vessel state machines.
- Video/frames are release derivatives; they never substitute for the executable worker-truth journey.

---

**Review verdict:** Revisions Requested

The governing architecture is sound; this is not a Block. The repair is a bounded truth-fold plus dependency/evidence-ownership pass so peers can self-select without implementing consumers before their real authorities.

### Continuation — live Stage 3/4/5 revalidation

Verified against the current issue bodies and GitHub dependency graph:

- ✅ #15239 now records #15240 as delivered, the parallel #15243/#15207 readiness rails, and the real consumption order.
- ✅ All eight requested dependency edges exist: #15241←#15207; #15251←#15245; #15247←#15248; #15250←#15246/#15207; #15252←#15245/#15207/#15248.
- ✅ Three-OS evidence ownership is explicit, and #15251 now carries the PR #15266 terminal-outcome scope guard.
- ✅ The three Stage-5 trap guards are present.
- ❌ One authority contradiction remains in #15207. Its live acceptance criteria still say: `No new timing/color literals: dock aliases + --fm-*/motion vocabulary only.` That still licenses `--fm-*` in the same ticket whose authority correction and intended shared-owner contract explicitly forbid `--fm-*` consumption in the workstation tree. Replace that AC with the app-neutral domain contract (the `--agent-dock-preview-*` aliases plus shared motion tokens); `--fm-*` may remain an AgentOS projection, not a shared-owner/workstation dependency.

**Continuation verdict:** Revisions Requested — one bounded #15207 body repair remains. No additional graph or implementation changes requested.

- 2026-07-16T14:49:27Z @tobiu referenced in commit `4c68529` - "docs(adr): ADR 0029 §2.8 — claim protocol, outcome machine, vessel lifecycle (#15240) (#15260)

* docs(adr): ADR 0029 §2.8 amendment — claim protocol, outcome machine, vessel lifecycle (#15240)

The multi-window choreography contracts graduated from D#15204 (quorum cycle-4b, epic
#15239) land as the record's own escalation path demands — the claim protocol CHANGES
DragCoordinator's target-resolution semantics, so it amends this ADR before any
consuming implementation:

- §2.8.1 the session-scoped gesture/claim protocol replacing first-intersecting
  registration-order resolution (stable workspace/zone identity, validity/expiry,
  deterministic tie/stale/no-claim — fail closed; the ≥3-window overlap witness as
  the binding falsifier), expressed dock-blind at the coordinator tier;
- §2.8.2 the finite gesture outcome machine with four invariants — including the
  named forbidden defect: onRemoteDropOut fires ONLY after a committed remote drop
  (a null onRemoteDrop must never retire a live source gesture);
- §2.8.3 vessel lifecycle on the Boolean admission truth (blocked popups never
  throw), close-as-post-commit-render-effect, and the explicit emptied-workspace
  registry disposition;
- §2.8.4 additive-dockLayout.v2 + ADR-0034 non-fork constraints and the merge order
  binding every consuming leaf to its §2.8 subsection.

Header summary + the §5 decomposition table gain the amendment's leaves. Docs-only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>

* docs(adr): §2.8.3 binds the full admission chain — bounded connect, generation, disconnect (#15240)

Cycle-1 review backfill: the graduated admission contract (the cycle-4c deposit,
carried by the version-bound family approval) is the FOUR-step chain, not the Boolean
alone — Boolean open → bounded connect admission (opened-but-never-connected fails
closed) → generation revalidation at connect (stale-generation vessels refused, never
adopted by successors) → disconnect correlation (workspace-set + outcome-state
resolution; onWindowDisconnect idempotent against committed outcomes). Without it the
acquisition leaf would reconstruct a graduation input from Discussion archaeology —
the exact requirement a decision record exists to remove.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Fable 5 <fable@anthropic.com>
